events.title: Fix voice training crash and reset logic
events.summary: The user debugged and fixed a crash in the My-Portrait and My-Meeting apps caused by improper state retention during voice training, particularly when recordings were too short. They implemented proper reset behavior on 'Start' to prevent locking, addressed 'got 0s' errors from audio format mismatches, and validated fixes in Xcode. The changes targeted files like 'Meeting 2026-06-18-20-10' and 'Meeting 2026-06-02-09-25', and used tools including claude --dangerously-skip-permissions and sourcekit-lsp.

events.title: Retrain speaker profiles and manage voiceprints
events.summary: The user executed a full speaker retraining workflow using stored audio and fallback meeting recordings, prioritizing 'speaker-training-audio/<id>.f32' files. They implemented upsert logic for speaker fingerprints, maintained ID and creation time, and used commands like 'retrainFromMeetings' and 'renameSpeakerProfile'. The process involved handling stale voiceprints, managing permissions with '--dangerously-skip-permissions', and targeting model readiness for 'Chinese ERes2NetV2' and 'Joy'.

events.title: Configure and test speech detection models
events.summary: The user configured and tested speech detection models including Silero VAD, FluidAudio VAD, and LSTM-based models, focusing on 'silero_vad.onnx' with parameters like 512 window and 64 context. They adjusted segmenters to use Energy (RMS), fixed 'noSpeechDetected' and 'noAudioFile' errors via LocalizedError, and re-transcribed audio files like 'Personal ai meeting 1.m4a'. The work involved 'RetranscriptionService.swift', 'Siler0.swift', and model version 'v5'.

events.title: Set up and deploy audio processing pipeline
events.summary: The user completed the setup and deployment of an end-to-end audio processing pipeline for MyMeeting, including linking Git repos, running Xcode builds, and deploying changes with a 10-minute timeout. They addressed SourceKit diagnostics, configured Qwen3-ASR for Chinese audio, and managed system-level audio capture using ScreenCaptureKit. The workflow involved 'MyMeeting.xcodeproj', 'ultracode', and 'sourcekit-lsp', with final evaluation of '2: Fine' for Claude's performance.

events.title: Implement error handling and UI feedback
events.summary: The user enhanced error handling in the audio processing stack by adding LocalizedError cases to the 'AudioError' enum for messages like 'noSpeechDetected' and 'sampleRateUnsupported'. They modified the 'isRecoverable' property to return true or false based on error type, and updated UI components like 'MemorySettingsView' and 'MemoriesView' to reflect state changes. The changes were tested in 'AudioChunk.swift' and 'RetranscriptionService.swift'.

events.title: Analyze personal evolution modeling
events.summary: The user analyzed the challenges of modeling long-term personal evolution using longitudinal data, focusing on distinguishing 'snapshot' vs 'movie' approaches to identity reconstruction. Key technical anchors included 10-year records, 'Stanford-style reflection', 'Opus 4.8 High', and the distinction between 20-, 25-, and 30-year self-states. The discussion emphasized identifying high-signal identity formation events from continuous data streams.

events.title: Evaluate speech recognition models
events.summary: The user assessed Qwen3-ASR, an upcoming open-source speech recognition model set for release in early 2026 under Apache 2.0. It supports 52 languages and dialects, including 22 Chinese dialects, achieves SOTA performance on Mandarin and singing benchmarks, and demonstrates 97.9% language identification accuracy across 30 languages—surpassing Whisper-lv3's 94.1%. The session also touched on philosophical implications of long-term personal data recording.

events.title: Test model phrase recall in Terminal
events.summary: The user tested a language model's ability to learn and recall specific phrases like '你说得对' through repeated input in the Terminal. Using tools such as caffeinate, claude, and userdb, they observed 91% accuracy on novel sentences and 100% on common ones, highlighting the model's limited generalization beyond explicitly trained phrases.

events.title: Transcribe and debug meeting audio
events.summary: The user transcribed multiple meetings including 'Meeting 2026-06-06_18-20-10' and 'Personal ai meeting 1.m4a', comparing FluidAudio and ONNX Silero models with 80% success rate. They debugged audio errors like 'AudioError error 6' and 'noSpeech', and managed RMS energy and bypass permissions.

events.title: Text processing and template refinement
events.summary: The user tested and refined a text completion system using tools like AxCleanUp and ultracode, addressing message truncation, Chinese character handling, and template editor functionality. They made changes in TemplateEditorView.swift to add state variables for summary description and participants, and tested Claude and Codex models under various input conditions, referencing 'writing-capture-researcher' and '115x29'.

events.title: Refine user input pipeline
events.summary: The user refined a message processing pipeline in Obsidian 1.12.7, focusing on splitting consecutive messages, handling send markers, and managing draft merges. Key elements included 'isSendClear', 'submit', 'endValue', 'mergePrefixDrafts', and 'U+FEFF' for deterministic message boundary detection. The work involved algorithmic adjustments to 'Pass 3 · AX 分支 | 确定性算法' and 'unit-session' structures.

events.title: Process canvas and session fusion
events.summary: The user executed document fusion and backlog processing in Obsidian 1.12.7, leveraging OCR modules, LLM embeddings, and compression for raw session and edit log handling. Activities included managing canvas documents, 'ax_cleaned' and 'ax_paste' files, and processing window 655 data to integrate user input from Canvas (e.g., Google Docs) into a unified session stream.

events.title: Configure headless Rime input method
events.summary: The user configured and tested a headless Rime input method using Librime, focusing on a 91% baseline accuracy for the 'WuSong' model. They validated the pipeline without code changes, feeding common phrases into /tmp/rime-test and referencing 'writing-capture-researcher' and 'userdb'.

events.title: Integrate Rime with AI and custom dictionaries
events.summary: The user integrated the Rime engine with AI providers like Claude and Groq, and customized word libraries using personal writing records. They configured dual-pinyin schemes, managed schema and switcher settings, and tested fusion with LLMs via 'lattice.c' and 'rime_api.h'.

events.title: Submitted final exam lab assignment
events.summary: The user submitted the 'FINAL EXAM MINI LAB' assignment via Google Chrome on June 4 at 4:02pm, late by five days past the May 29 deadline. The assignment, worth 100 points and intended for students with absences, was accessed through chccs.instructure.com and involved downloading 'FINAL EXAM MINI LAB.docx' and using the 'New Attempt' feature to resubmit.
